Politics
09:54, 14-Apr-2026
CGTN
, Updated 12:02, 14-Apr-2026To Lam, general secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam Central Committee and president of Vietnam, arrived in Beijing on Tuesday for a state visit to China until Friday.
China is the first country To Lam visits following his recent election as Vietnamese president.
At a recent regular press conference, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said that China hopes that through the visit, both China and Vietnam will carry forward the traditional friendship, continue to deepen the comprehensive strategic cooperative partnership, advance the building of an even higher level of community with a shared future that carries strategic significance, jointly promote the socialist cause in the world, and together maintain regional and world peace, stability and development.
(Cover: A plane carrying To Lam, general secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam Central Committee and president of Vietnam, arrives in Beijing, April 14, 2026. /CMG)
Source(s): Xinhua News Agency

corpus.title: Why did Vietnam's To Lam fast-track trip to China after taking office?
corpus.signal_text:
Why did Vietnam's To Lam fast-track trip to China after taking office?

Vietnam's President To Lam's fast-tracked overseas trip after assuming office reflects Vietnam's prioritization of China amid global uncertainty and underscores the stabilizing role of China鈥揤ietnam relations.

corpus.title: Vietnam appoints new ambassador to North Korea after envoy鈥檚 lengthy term
corpus.signal_text:
Vietnam appoints new ambassador to North Korea after envoy鈥檚 lengthy term

Vietnam recently appointed a new ambassador to North Korea, replacing its previous envoy after a longer-than-usual tenure in Pyongyang. Vietnamese President To Lam on Tuesday designated as ambassador to the DPRK Doan Khanh Tam, the deputy director of the Foreign Ministry鈥檚 Information and Documentation Center at the Diplomatic Academy, according to Hanoi鈥檚 state-run Vietnam News [鈥

corpus.title: Vietnam adds over 2 square km of land in South China Sea, US report says
corpus.signal_text:
Vietnam adds over 2 square km of land in South China Sea, US report says

Vietnam has expanded its outposts in the South China Sea by hundreds of acres over the past year, according to a new report, as Hanoi and Beijing race to reinforce competing territorial claims through land reclamation. Vietnam has added about 534 acres (2.16 square km) of land in the Spratly Islands, bringing its total reclaimed area to roughly 2,771 acres (11.2 square km), according to the Washington-based Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative. While Hanoi had appeared to be narrowing the gap..

corpus.title: Vietnam, Sri Lanka upgrade ties to comprehensive partnership
corpus.signal_text:
Vietnam, Sri Lanka upgrade ties to comprehensive partnership

Vietnam's Party General Secretary and President To Lam and Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissanayake met with the press on May 8, during which they announced the outcomes of their just-ended talks in Colombo, including the upgrade of the bilateral ties to a Comprehensive Partnership.

corpus.title: Vietnam's top leader To Lam welcomed in New Delhi with 21-gun salute
corpus.signal_text:
Vietnam's top leader To Lam welcomed in New Delhi with 21-gun salute

India's President Droupadi Murmu and Prime Minister Narendra Modi on May 6 co-chaired an official welcome ceremony with a 21-cannon salute for Vietnam's Party General Secretary and President To Lam, who is on a three-day state visit to the country.
